prerequisites: A Salesforce org (production or sandbox) with API access enabled and a user account that has permission to query the objects you want to sync.
A connected app (or external client app) configured for OAuth 2.0, which provides the consumer key and consumer secret used as client_id and client_secret.
An OAuth refresh token obtained by authorizing that connected app against your org (the refresh_token field), so pm can mint access tokens without interactive login.

If you connect to a sandbox rather than production, knowledge that the org is a sandbox so you can set is_sandbox to true (authorization runs against test.salesforce.com instead of login.salesforce.com).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Salesforce REST API authorizes clients via the OAuth 2.0 protocol through a connected app. pm uses the connected app's consumer key (client_id) and consumer secret (client_secret) together with a long-lived refresh_token to obtain access tokens. This maps to the connector's required fields client_id, client_secret (secret), and refresh_token (secret). The auth_type field selects this client flow, and is_sandbox determines whether tokens are minted against the production or sandbox login host.

setupSteps.title: Create a connected app and enable OAuth
setupSteps.body: In Salesforce Setup, create a connected app (or an external client app) and enable its OAuth settings. After saving, Salesforce generates a consumer key and consumer secret for the app; these become the connector's client_id and client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Authorize the app and capture a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth 2.0 flow against your org so the user grants the connected app access. Salesforce returns an access token and a refresh token in exchange for the authorization grant. Save the refresh token as the connector's refresh_token value; pm uses it to mint fresh access tokens on each run.

setupSteps.title: Configure required credential fields
setupSteps.body: Set client_id (consumer key), client_secret (consumer secret), and refresh_token on the source. Set auth_type to select the OAuth client flow. These three credential fields are all required.

setupSteps.title: Set the org host and starting point
setupSteps.body: Set is_sandbox to true if you are connecting to a sandbox org (authorization and API calls target test.salesforce.com instead of login.salesforce.com); leave it false for production. Optionally set start_date to bound the initial sync window.

setupSteps.title: Tune extraction behavior (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set force_use_bulk_api to prefer the Bulk API for large objects, streams_criteria to filter which objects (streams) are synced, and lookback_window or stream_slice_step to control incremental cursor overlap and slice sizing. All of these are optional.

sources.title: Salesforce REST API Developer Guide: Authorization Through External Client Apps or Connected Apps and OAuth 2.0
